sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old male, presents with a history of injuring his right knee at a trampoline park a week ago.
He reports hearing a snap and experiencing immediate pain.
He denies any previous knee injuries or conditions.
He has been experiencing pain on the outside of the knee, with a slight limp, but denies any difficulty walking.
He has been taking Tylenol for the pain, but it has provided minimal relief.
He has a history of hypertension, which he has been managing with 20mg of lisinopril daily, although his blood pressure is still slightly elevated.
He also has a history of diabetes, for which he takes 500mg of metformin daily, but has not been consistent with his blood glucose monitoring, checking it only 4-5 times a week.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension and diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms related to his knee injury or other medical conditions.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 180/80 mmHg.
Physical exam: The patient's knee examination revealed ecchymosis and edema on the lateral aspect, but no effusion.
He had a slight limp, but was able to bend and straighten his knee.
The rest of the examination was unremarkable.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain of the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Lateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; Diabetes
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling associated with the lateral ligament strain.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the area and prevent further injury.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension and order an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1c to evaluate long-term blood glucose control and a lipid panel to assess lipid profiles.
Follow-up: I will schedule a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the x-ray and echocardiogram and to adjust the patient's treatment plan as needed.
